title: gagea pygmaea subsp. distans
intro: Gagea pygmaea subsp. distans, also known as the dwarf gagea or a rare subspecies of the gagea pygmaea, is a small, bulbous perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, which has led to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of gagea pygmaea subsp. distans are thought to include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are typically made by drying and processing the plant's bulbs and flowers, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or other supplements.
